message.content:
Research the KEY CUSTOMERS, CUSTOMER CONCENTRATION, and SUBSIDIARIES/JVs of Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd, an Indian Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS) company in Mysore.

Use WebSearch then WebFetch on authoritative sources (vinyasit.com, annual reports, screener.in, moneycontrol, news like Economic Times / Business Standard).

Gather:
1. Key customers (Bharat Electronics / BEL, other defense PSUs, export/overseas customers).
2. Customer concentration — what % of revenue comes from top customer(s)? Is BEL the dominant customer? Concentration risk.
3. Subsidiaries / JVs, especially any US subsidiary named "Vinyas Innovative Technologies Inc." — confirm existence, location, purpose.

You have WebSearch and WebFetch tools. Run several searches and fetch the most authoritative pages.

Return a concise markdown bullet list of findings. After EACH fact put the exact source URL in parentheses. Distinguish confirmed facts from estimates. Flag anything unverified. Do NOT write files — return findings as your text output.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding Vinyas Innovative Technologies Inc US subsidiary:

## Subsidiary Establishment

Vinyas Innovative Technologies announced the incorporation of a wholly owned subsidiary, Vinyas Innovative Technologies Inc., in the United States. The subsidiary belongs to the Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS), Defense, Aerospace, and allied sectors.

## Timeline and Status

The process for subscription to the share capital of the subsidiary is expected to be completed by May 2026. The subsidiary is yet to commence its preliminary business activities.

## Strategic Purpose

This strategic move is aimed at expanding the company's presence in the US market and strengthening relationships with global customers. The establishment of this subsidiary is part of Vinyas Innovative Technologies' efforts to facilitate international business operations, enhance customer engagement, and support business development activities.

## Parent Company Financial Performance

For context, Vinyas Innovative Technologies reported a 58.92% YoY increase in FY26 net profit to ₹30.87 crore, driven by a 29.45% rise in revenue to ₹514.32 crore.

The search results do not contain a specific annual report for the US subsidiary, as it was only recently incorporated and has not yet commenced business activities.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Vinyas Innovative Technologies' subsidiaries, joint ventures, and defense customers:

## Joint Ventures

Vinyas Innovative Technologies Limited has formed a joint venture with an Israel-based defence technology company to expand in the defence and aerospace sector. The company will invest ₹2.6 crore for a 26% stake in the domestic entity, which will focus on manufacturing and system integration. The joint venture with the Israeli defense company is for manufacturing defense systems in India.

## Subsidiaries

The company has incorporated a wholly-owned subsidiary in the US to pursue manufacturing opportunities there.

## Defense Customers

Vinyas has been partnering with world's best OEMs of aerospace & defence industry for over two decades and is successfully catering to the mission-critical electronics manufacturing requirements as well as military supply chain needs in land, sea and air. Additionally, the company has partnered with Indian Defence companies for electronics manufacturing requirements.

The company's operations span aerospace, industrial, and medical sectors, with significant export revenue from European and US markets.

input.prompt: Extract all details about Vinyas Innovative Technologies' key customers, customer concentration (% of revenue from top customers, BEL/Bharat Electronics dependence), export customers, subsidiaries, and joint ventures from this FY2026 earnings call.
Extract all details about the US subsidiary Vinyas Innovative Technologies Inc: name, location, purpose, ownership, share capital, timeline.
Extract details about Vinyas Innovative Technologies' joint venture with the Israel-based company: name of partner, stake %, investment amount, purpose, location.

content.content:
# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Joint Venture Details

**Partner Company:** Israel-based defence technology company (name not specified in article)

**Ownership Structure:** 
- Vinyas Innovative Technologies: 26%
- Israeli partner: 74%

**Investment Amount:** ₹2.6 crore (contributed by Vinyas)

**Purpose:** The collaboration focuses on "manufacturing, assembly, system integration, maintenance, lifecycle support, and related activities within the defence and aerospace sector."

**Location:** India (described as a "domestic joint venture")

**Additional Details:**
- Structured under a Joint Venture and Shareholders' Agreement
- Equity shares issued at ₹10 face value per share
- Subject to regulatory and statutory approvals
- Includes standard governance provisions such as board representation and management participation
- The company confirmed "there is no conflict of interest arising from this arrangement"

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Inc. - US Subsidiary Details

**Name:** Vinyas Innovative Technologies Inc.

**Location:** United States

**Purpose:** The subsidiary aims to expand the parent company's presence in the US market and strengthen relationships with global customers. It will "facilitate international business operations, enhance customer engagement, and support business development activities" while coordinating supply chains and enabling strategic overseas expansion.

**Ownership:** 100% wholly owned subsidiary with complete parental control

**Share Capital:** The subscription process was "expected to be completed by May 2026," acquired through share capital subscription rather than cash consideration.

**Timeline:** Incorporated in 2026; business activities had not yet commenced at the time of the announcement on May 29, 2026.

**Industry Focus:** Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS), Defense, Aerospace, and allied sectors

**Additional Note:** This transaction was classified as a related party transaction, with parent company promoters' interest limited to their existing shareholding in Vinyas Innovative Technologies.

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies - Customer & Partnership Details from FY2026 Earnings

## Key Customers & Concentration
The earnings call transcript provided does not include specific names of major customers or customer concentration percentages. There is no mention of BEL (Bharat Electronics) dependence in the available content.

## Sector Distribution
- **Defense sector:** Represents "85% to 90% of the order book"
- **Other sectors:** Aerospace, industrial, and medical segments operate on rolling forecasts with long-term orders primarily from defense and aerospace

## Export Markets
- **Export contribution:** Approximately 50% of FY26 revenue
- **Key markets:** Europe and the US
- **Future outlook:** Company expects to maintain a 50-50 split between domestic and export revenue

## Strategic Partnerships & Subsidiaries

**Joint Venture:**
- Partnership with a leading Israeli defense company for manufacturing defense systems in India

**Subsidiary:**
- Wholly-owned subsidiary incorporated in the US to pursue manufacturing opportunities

## Supply Chain Dependencies
- Approximately 10-15% of supplies sourced from Israel, creating potential geopolitical exposure

**Note:** The article does not provide detailed information about specific customer names, revenue concentration from top clients, or BEL relationship specifics.

## Company Overview

Vinyas Innovative Technologies was incorporated in 2001 as a leading provider of design, engineering and electronics manufacturing services. The company was founded in the year 2000 with a vision to create global presence for India in the Electronics Manufacturing industry.

## Location and Operations

The company is located in Koorgalli Industrial Area, Mysuru, and is engaged in manufacturing and supplying a wide array of PCB Assembly from Mysore, Karnataka, India.

## Services Offered

Vinyas Innovative Technologies provides design, engineering, and electronics manufacturing services, offering PCB assembly, box build, system integration, DFM, DFT, and prototyping solutions. The company is a preferred partner for Global OEMs and ODMs by offering end-to-end solutions ranging from Design for Manufacturability, Supply Chain Management, PCB assembly, advanced test solutions, Product Integration and after-market support.

## BEL/Bharat Electronics Connection

The search results do not contain any specific information about Vinyas Innovative Technologies being a supplier or vendor to BEL (Bharat Electronics Limited). While Vinyas is clearly a PCB assembly and electronics manufacturing services provider based in Mysore, the search results don't mention any direct business relationship with BEL.

Vinyas Innovative Technologies holds an estimated order book of Rs 1,488 crore as of April 2024, to be executed over the next 3–4 years, with a strong client base, primarily in the defense sector. The defense sector contributes about 85% to 90% of the company's order book. Recently, Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd. has secured multiple purchase orders totaling over Rs 635 crore from domestic and international clients in the last 3 months.

Established in 2001, Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd. (VITL) specializes in electronics manufacturing services (EMS) for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and original design manufacturers (ODMs), with core products being printed circuit boards (PCBs) offering end-to-end solutions, including design, supply chain management, PCB assembly, advanced testing, product integration, and after-market support.

Based on my web search results, I can provide you with information about Vinyas Innovative Technologies' business and customer segments, though the search results did not return a comprehensive list of specific named customers from the IPO prospectus.

## Company Overview

Vinyas Innovative Technologies Limited, established in 2001, provides design, engineering, and electronics manufacturing services to global Original Equipment Manufacturers and Original Design Manufacturers in the electronic industry, with over 20 years of experience.

## Business Focus and Sectors

Vinyas is a leading Electronics Manufacturing Service provider specializing in PCB assembly for the aerospace, medical, and defence markets, and is an AS 9100-D certified firm that has set benchmarks in the electronics industry for these sectors.

In H1 FY2026, the Defence & Aerospace segment contributed 78.5% of total revenue.

## Revenue and Orders

Vinyas reported a net profit of ₹30.87 crore for the financial year ended March 31, 2026, a 58.92% increase from ₹19.42 crore in the previous year, with revenue from operations surging 29.45% to ₹514.32 crore for FY26, compared to ₹396.64 crore in FY25, driven by the company's defence and aerospace segments.

## IPO Details

The Vinyas Innovative Technologies Limited IPO ran from September 27, 2023, to October 3, 2023, with the SME IPO anticipated to be listed on October 11, 2023.

Unfortunately, the search results did not provide a specific list of major customers such as Bharat Electronics, HAL, Tata, or Honeywell from the IPO prospectus document. The prospectus references (RHP and DRHP) were mentioned but the detailed customer lists from these documents were not available in the search results.

content.text:
The NSE PDF consistently times out. I have sufficient corroborated data from multiple sources. Here are my findings.

---

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd — Customers, Concentration & Subsidiaries/JVs

## Key Customers

- **Bharat Electronics Ltd (BEL) is a confirmed major customer.** Vinyas received a ₹43.5 crore purchase order from BEL's Chennai units, and had earlier received a ₹26.25 crore order from BEL's Bengaluru and Chennai units. BEL is described as the order source in financial-media coverage. (https://tradebrains.in/features/microcap-stock-jumps-8-after-securing-an-order-worth-43-5-cr-from-bel/)
- **Customer base is concentrated in Defence & Aerospace,** working with Indian defence companies/PSUs and global aerospace-defence OEMs. Defence contributes roughly **85%–90% of the order book**; in H1 FY26 Defence & Aerospace was **78.5% of revenue**, and in FY23 it was **82.3%** (₹19,303.94 lakh of total). (https://ca.finance.yahoo.com/news/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-nse-010216958.html) (https://www.bitget.com/stock/nse-vinyas/what-is)
- **Export/overseas customers are significant — about 50% of FY26 revenue,** primarily global OEMs/ODMs in **Europe and the US** across aerospace, medical, industrial and telecom. Specific overseas customer names were not disclosed in the sources reviewed. (https://ca.finance.yahoo.com/news/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-nse-010216958.html)
- Order book is large and defence-led: reported around **₹1,062 crore** (with ₹1,488 crore cited at an earlier date), executable over 3–4 years. (https://tradebrains.in/features/semiconductor-stock-jumps-4-after-receiving-an-order-worth-54-cr-for-pcbs/ — via search summary)

## Customer Concentration / Concentration Risk

- **Confirmed: heavy single-customer reliance.** The IPO prospectus (RHP) risk factors state the company's "revenue is significantly reliant on a single customer, which represents a substantial portion of its income." (https://www.finowings.com/IPO/vinyas-innovative-technologies-limited-ipo)
- **Sector concentration is also high:** Defence + Industrial combined exceed ~90% of revenue, exposing the company to government procurement cycles and the risk of losing a major contract. (https://www.moneyworks4me.com/indianstocks/small-cap/capital-goods-industrial-consumables/electronics-components/vinyas-innova-techno/company-info)
- **UNVERIFIED — the exact top-customer % was not found, nor was it confirmed that BEL specifically is the single dominant customer.** BEL is clearly a large/repeat customer, but I could not confirm from authoritative disclosures that BEL is *the* single customer driving the prospectus's concentration warning. The exact top-1/top-5 revenue percentages are in the RHP/annual report (the NSE RHP PDF and H1 FY26 investor presentation could not be parsed — PDF was binary/compressed and the NSE link repeatedly timed out). This figure should be verified directly in those filings.
  - Note: A search result citing an SEC EDGAR filing ("Customer A 15%, Customer B 13%") refers to an unrelated US company and does **not** apply to Vinyas — disregard it.

## Subsidiaries / Joint Ventures

- **US subsidiary CONFIRMED: "Vinyas Innovative Technologies Inc."** — a 100% wholly-owned subsidiary incorporated in the **United States**, announced **29 May 2026**. Purpose: expand US-market presence, strengthen relationships with global customers, coordinate supply chains and support business development in EMS / Defence / Aerospace. Share-capital subscription was expected to complete by **May 2026**; business activities had **not yet commenced** at announcement. Treated as a related-party transaction. (https://www.businessupturn.com/business/vinyas-innovative-technologies-establishes-wholly-owned-subsidiary-in-the-usa) (https://www.vinyasit.com/)
- **Israel defence JV CONFIRMED (domestic JV in India):** Vinyas entered a JV with an **Israel-based defence-technology company**, taking a **26% stake** with an investment of **₹2.6 crore** (shares at ₹10 face value); the Israeli partner holds the remaining 74%. Scope: manufacturing, assembly, system integration, maintenance, lifecycle support and related defence/aerospace activities in India, under a JV & Shareholders' Agreement. (https://www.businessupturn.com/business/vinyas-innovative-technologies-enters-strategic-joint-venture-with-israel-based-company/) (https://ca.finance.yahoo.com/news/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-nse-010216958.html)
  - **UNVERIFIED:** the Israeli partner's name and the JV entity's name were not disclosed in the sources reviewed.
- **Related supply-chain note:** ~10–15% of Vinyas's supplies are sourced from Israel, a stated geopolitical exposure. (https://ca.finance.yahoo.com/news/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-nse-010216958.html)

## Company Snapshot (context)
- Mysore (Koorgalli)-based EMS provider, incorporated 2001, AS9100-D certified; NSE Emerge SME IPO Sept–Oct 2023; FY26 revenue ₹514.32 cr (+29.45%), net profit ₹30.87 cr (+58.92%). (https://ca.finance.yahoo.com/news/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-nse-010216958.html) (https://www.vinyasit.com/company-overview/)

## Items to verify directly in primary filings
1. Exact top-customer revenue % and whether BEL is the single dominant customer — in the RHP (https://archives.nseindia.com/emerge/corporates/content/Vinyas_RHP.pdf) and latest annual report.
2. Names of overseas/export customers.
3. Name of the Israeli JV partner and JV entity.

The RHP PDF and H1 FY26 investor presentation (https://nsearchives.nseindia.com/corporate/VINYAS_06112025180228_Investor_Presentation_H1_202526.pdf) are the authoritative sources for items 1–3 but could not be machine-parsed in this session (binary PDF / repeated timeouts).
